assumption_text: The April civilian denominator is limited to civilians physically present inside Fallujah city; Ramadi and other Anbar fighting are excluded. Directly assigned cordon/blocking forces are counted only in the military denominator.
rationale: Using province- or country-wide civilian populations would violate the episode's city-level geography and artificially depress civilian mortality rates.

assumption_text: Average April civilian presence is modeled as 180k / 225k / 275k over the 26-day episode, using pre-siege population anchors of 250k-300k and at least 60,000 people fleeing by 9-10 April.
rationale: No complete displacement register was located, but the denominator must fall below the city baseline once the siege and partial evacuations begin.

assumption_text: April military person-months reflect combined Coalition, Iraqi partner, and insurgent exposure, averaging roughly 5.1k / 6.3k / 8.4k active combatants over the episode.
rationale: The DR denominator for military/combatants should cover all organized armed personnel exposed in or directly assigned to the battle theater, not only one side.

assumption_text: The core April split accepts IBC's case-specific allocation of mixed local deaths into civilians and insurgents and carries no residual unknown-status deaths.
rationale: IBC x360 is the most transparent public reconstruction available. Rejecting its allocation would leave a larger unknown-status pool and prevent a clean core DR estimate.

assumption_text: The second-battle civilian exposure window ends on 2004-12-23, because civilians began re-entering on 2004-12-24. Military isolation/assault positions directly assigned to the city remain in the military denominator.
rationale: This keeps the civilian denominator tied to the closed-city combat phase instead of mixing it with the later re-peopling phase.

assumption_text: Average November-December civilian presence is modeled as roughly 9.6k / 20.4k / 38.3k over 47 days, bracketing the conflicting '<500', 15k-30k, 30k-60k, and 50k-60k source claims.
rationale: The central uncertainty in the second battle is how many civilians actually remained in the city when the assault and rubble-collapse deaths occurred.

assumption_text: Second-battle military person-months reflect combined Coalition, Iraqi partner, and insurgent exposure, averaging roughly 9.6k / 11.8k / 14.4k active combatants over the episode.
rationale: The total armed exposure should include 10k-12k directly assigned Coalition/Iraqi forces and roughly 3k-4.5k insurgents, with some late-November redeployment.

assumption_text: Official military fatality totals that run through 2004-12-31 are used as upper-bound numerator inputs for an episode whose civilian exposure window ends on 2004-12-23.
rationale: The official histories do not provide a clean second-battle military death cut precisely at the start of civilian re-entry, so the range is used to absorb the mismatch.

assumption_text: Indirect deaths from deprivation, denial of medical care, or post-assault collapse are excluded from the core DR even when sources mention them contextually.
rationale: The project definition requires the baseline DR to use direct violent deaths only.
